package handler

import (
	"errors"
	"net/http"
	"strings"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

// Positive scenario for handler_switches_on_error_strings.
// Rule intent: Handlers should branch on typed/sentinel errors (errors.Is, errors.As),
// not on err.Error() string matching which is fragile and breaks with refactoring.
// Anti-pattern: switch err.Error() or strings.Contains(err.Error(), "...") for control flow.

type UserHandler struct{}

func (h *UserHandler) GetUser(c *gin.Context) {
	id := c.Param("id")
	user, err := findUser(id)
	if err != nil {
		// Anti-pattern: switching on error string instead of using typed errors
		switch err.Error() {
		case "not found":
			c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, gin.H{"error": "user not found"})
		case "duplicate key value violates unique constraint":
			c.JSON(http.StatusConflict, gin.H{"error": "duplicate user"})
		case "connection refused":
			c.JSON(http.StatusBadGateway, gin.H{"error": "database unavailable"})
		case "context deadline exceeded":
			c.JSON(http.StatusGatewayTimeout, gin.H{"error": "request timed out"})
		default:
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "internal error"})
		}
		return
	}

	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"user": user})
}

func (h *UserHandler) DeleteUser(c *gin.Context) {
	id := c.Param("id")
	err := deleteUser(id)
	if err != nil {
		// Anti-pattern: string matching on error messages
		msg := err.Error()
		switch {
		case strings.Contains(msg, "foreign key constraint"):
			c.JSON(http.StatusConflict, gin.H{"error": "user has associated records"})
		case strings.Contains(msg, "not found"):
			c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, gin.H{"error": "user not found"})
		case strings.Contains(msg, "permission denied"):
			c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, gin.H{"error": "insufficient permissions"})
		default:
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "delete failed"})
		}
		return
	}

	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"deleted": true})
}

func findUser(id string) (interface{}, error) {
	return nil, errors.New("not found")
}

func deleteUser(id string) error {
	return errors.New("foreign key constraint failed")
}
